title: Agent Control Contract — declarative agent config file
description: The declarative, language-neutral agent config-file surface of the Agent Control Contract (v1). It carries ONLY verbose, environment-stable STRUCTURAL config: the model/limits knobs, the MCP-server inventory, declared subscriptions, A2A delegation peers, the log level, and declared intelligence headers. It is secret-free — only structural REFERENCES live here, via {{secret:NAME}} / {{secret-file:PATH}} templates (resolved values NEVER land in the file or a log). The file is the lowest non-default precedence layer: built-in default < FILE < env < flag; list keys (mcp_servers/subscribe/a2a_peers) are REPLACED at the file boundary but repeatable flags (--mcp/--subscribe/--a2a-peer) ADD to the file's list. Validated/exported by `<agent> --validate-config` (exit 0 valid / 2 invalid) and `<agent> --config-schema`, and served live at agent://config/schema + agent://config/effective. This file is the CONTRACT VIEW: it additionally DECLARES the restart-only mode discriminator (mode/interval/cron) so a consumer that finds them in a file emits a WARNING rather than a hard deny_unknown_fields error — the strict serde mirror (only the reloadable file keys) is published separately. Extracted from agent RFC 0017 (declarative-config-and-hot-reload), RFC 0008 (modes), RFC 0011 (precedence/secrets/validate-at-startup), RFC 0020 (a2a peers), and the reference source crates/agent/src/config_file.rs (ConfigFile/LimitsFile/McpServerFile/A2aPeerFile, config_schema()) + crates/agent/src/config.rs (Mode enum, env/flag handling).

x-provisional.P6: The agentctl design-record §14 marked `--config-schema` / `--validate-config` as UNBUILT. This is RESOLVED IN SOURCE, contrary to that note: crates/agent/src/config_file.rs::config_schema() is a hand-written serde_json::Value (no schemars — a forbidden dependency), wired at config.rs (the --config-schema and --validate-config entrypoints) and kept drift-free against the ConfigFile struct by tests schema_properties_match_struct_fields / schema_contract_version_matches_manifest. This schema is extracted faithfully from that source plus RFC 0017; treat it as provisional pending the design-record reconciliation.
x-provisional.mode-in-file: mode/interval/cron are RESTART-ONLY (env/flag), NOT reloadable file keys in the reference ConfigFile struct (which is serde deny_unknown_fields over only the reloadable keys). This contract view DECLARES them so they are recognized (a typo'd reloadable key is still exit 2; a restart-only key present in the file is a WARNING, and changing one on a LIVE reload is REJECTED). See x-restart-only.

x-versioning.posture: CLOSED object (additionalProperties:false), mirroring serde deny_unknown_fields — a typo'd key (max_token vs max_tokens) is caught at the gate as exit 2. This deliberately DIFFERS from the manifest/metrics OPEN-object additive-tolerance rule, because this is the config-INPUT surface (catch operator typos), not a discovery surface.
x-versioning.additive: A new OPTIONAL field is an additive change → contract MINOR bump.
x-versioning.breaking: Removing/renaming/narrowing a field, or making an optional field required → contract MAJOR bump. A consumer refuses only an unknown MAJOR (config_version / x-contract-version major).

x-precedence: built-in default < FILE (this schema) < env (AGENT_*) < flag. List keys mcp_servers/subscribe/a2a_peers are REPLACED at the file boundary; repeatable --mcp/--subscribe/--a2a-peer flags ADD to the file list.

x-restart-only.note: env/flag ONLY — declared here so the file validator can WARN (not error) and a live reload can REJECT a change. Reloadable bounds for the loop live in limits.deadline_secs.

fields.cron: --cron (5-field UTC cron; needs the `cron` build feature; only valid with mode=schedule)

config_version.description: OPTIONAL. The file's OWN pin to a contract major the operator authored against (distinct from x-contract-version, which is the schema's version). A consumer refuses only an unknown MAJOR.

intelligence.description: Reloadable. The primary intelligence transport/endpoint — the ordered intelligence endpoint list URI (RFC 0018 §3.1). File-settable + RELOADABLE so a ConfigMap update can repoint the endpoint list as a hot-swap (RFC 0018 §5): the reload fans ctrl/swap_intel to in-flight work and re-points new spawns. The transport SCHEME is data, not a secret; the per-endpoint credential is NEVER inline here (env/_FILE only). Flag --intelligence; env AGENT_INTELLIGENCE. Source = config_file.rs (ConfigFile.intelligence).

model_swap.description: Reloadable. The model hot-swap policy (RFC 0018 §5.3): finish-on-old | restart-turn. Validated against config::SwapPolicy. Flag --model-swap; env AGENT_MODEL_SWAP. Source = config_file.rs (ConfigFile.model_swap).

model.description: Reloadable. Operator-declared model id param (NOT the intelligence transport/endpoint — that is the reloadable `intelligence` file key). Never a secret. Flag --model; env AGENT_MODEL.

max_tokens.description: Reloadable. Token budget per turn. Reference type is u64. Flag --max-tokens; env AGENT_MAX_TOKENS.

mcp_servers.description: Reloadable. Default []. The MCP server inventory; one object == one `--mcp name=cmd ... --mcp-tags ...` flag group. Merge is REPLACE at the file layer, but repeatable --mcp flags ADD to this list.

subscribe.description: Reloadable. Default []. Declared subscriptions (reactive mode). Each string == one `--subscribe URI`. Repeatable --subscribe flags ADD to this list.

a2a_peers.description: Reloadable. Default []. Declared remote-A2A delegation peers; each == one `--a2a-peer name=endpoint` (RFC 0020). Present in the reference source (A2aPeerFile); absent from the RFC 0017 §3.3 example — source wins.

log_level.description: Reloadable LIVE. Flag --log-level; env AGENT_LOG_LEVEL. Enum order per the reference config_schema() (trace..error).

intelligence_headers.description: Reloadable. Declared intelligence HTTP headers. Header NAMES are structural; each VALUE is EITHER a literal header value OR a secret-ref TEMPLATE ({{secret:NAME}} / {{secret-file:PATH}}). Both serialize as a plain string, so the literal-vs-template distinction is a runtime/validation concern, not a schema type. A credential-shaped header name (see x-secret-shaped-names) MUST carry a secret reference, not an inline literal, or validation fails exit 2.

mode.description: RESTART-ONLY (env AGENT_MODE / flag --mode) — declared here only so a consumer WARNs rather than hard-rejects when it appears in a file; changing it on a LIVE reload is REJECTED. The execution mode: `once` runs the instruction to a terminal status then exits; `loop` repeats per --interval; `reactive` processes subscribe[] events (no terminal run-report); `schedule` is per-fire-identical-to-once driven by --interval or --cron. See the per-mode discriminator in allOf.

interval.description: RESTART-ONLY (flag --interval). The loop/schedule interval as a human duration (e.g. "5m"). Valid with mode=loop or mode=schedule; for mode=schedule, one of interval or cron is required.

cron.description: RESTART-ONLY (flag --cron). A 5-field UTC cron expression; honoured only in `cron`-feature builds (the production path is an external CronJob -> mode=once). Only valid with mode=schedule.

allOf.$comment: Per-mode discriminated params (RFC 0008; config.rs validate()). mode=schedule requires interval OR cron.
cron is only valid with mode=schedule.

Limits.description: Reloadable bounds on the model loop, applied to NEW spawns/turns (in-flight children keep their minted budgets). NOTE: RFC 0017 §3.3 also listed tree_token_budget + max_total_subagents here, but the reference LimitsFile struct exposes ONLY these three as file keys — source wins.

deadline_secs.description: Whole seconds. Flag --deadline. A finite deadline is mandatory at resolution (RFC 0011 §3.3). Reference type u64.

McpServer.description: One MCP server. name/command/argv map to a `--mcp name="command argv..."` flag group; tags is the RFC 0012 §3.1 glob->tags wire.

transport.description: stdio (default) is the only transport the reference client speaks today; unix is accepted in the schema.

env_passthrough.description: Env var NAMES only — values come from the process env, never inline (no secrets in the file).

tags.description: Glob pattern -> trifecta tags (RFC 0012 §3.1). Keys are GLOB patterns (e.g. "*", "read_*"), NOT literal tool names. An untagged server defaults to untrusted_input.

A2aPeer.description: One A2A delegation peer — maps to `--a2a-peer name=endpoint` (RFC 0020).

HeaderValue.description: An intelligence header value: EITHER a literal string OR a secret-ref template. The secret-ref forms are {{secret:NAME}} (env/loaded secret by name) and {{secret-file:PATH}} (mounted-secret file). Both are plain strings on the wire; the schema type cannot distinguish them. A credential-shaped header name MUST use a template (validator enforces, exit 2).
